message:
fix(fr-command): DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE permissive parse + upstream wording (partial br-frankenredis-1pe7)

Upstream debug.c::debugCommand:
  - Parses SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE's argument with a permissive atoi-style
    path: any non-zero integer enables, 0 disables, anything else
    truncates at the first non-digit (so "0.5" → 0). Ours previously
    rejected values outside {0,1} as syntax error.
  - Wrong-arity reply is "ERR unknown subcommand or wrong number of
    arguments for 'SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE'. Try DEBUG HELP." — we were
    emitting the generic DEBUG wrong-arity.

core_debug XFAIL list drops 5 cases now that SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE
matches upstream: debug_set_active_expire_{float_value,
invalid_value, large_value, negative_value, wrong_arity}.
core_debug live oracle: 38/38 pass (was 33/33 with 5 XFAIL).

Fixture sync: core_debug.json + core_server.json expectations for
the SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE arity/value cases.

DEBUG DIGEST / DIGEST-VALUE / DEBUG OBJECT reply-field parity
remain on br-frankenredis-1pe7 as the substantive follow-up work.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
